author: terencechain
content:
The only downside is that having two substantial additions in parallel can make merge conflicts a pain to deal with on the client side. That said, it prob will not be too bad. From a quick look, the overlapping areas seem fairly isolated. EIP-7928 appears on beacon state, the payload, and the Engine API call

Is there a consensus-specs PR that reflects the latest progress on merging EIP-7928 into the gloas spec folder? I can take this on in a day or two once the spec is a bit more finalized <@1126229231572094976>

author: barnabasbusa
content: having two unstable devnets will result one stable one ?

author: barnabasbusa
content: ideally bals and epbs gets stable on their own and then we merge the two

author: sproul
content: We are updating LH to follow _Nico's approach_. Could run test vectors if someone builds them from the branch 🙏

author: sproul
content: Anyone else looking at block production in the new paradigm? Seems like we need to execute `process_parent_execution_payload` while proposing, before forming the bid. We need to process the parent payload to know what the withdrawals should be for the block we're proposing, right?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: yeah i m trying to wrap my head around that too, `process_parent_execution_payload` adds the builders pending payment to the builder payment withdrawal queue and adds partial withdrawals too via `process_withdrawal_request`. I am not sure if using `get_expected_withdrawals(state)` in `prepare_execution_payload` is right?

author: sproul
content:
yeah I don't think it is, I think we need to make `process_parent_execution_payload` optional in block processing, and do something like:

- if deciding to build on parent block's bid: run `process_parent_execution_payload` on state
- compute withdrawals for EL interaction/bid creation
- process beacon block (now including bid we just built), but with `process_parent_execution_payload` _NOT RUNNING_ if we are building on parent block's bid (don't want to run twice)
- compute state root for beacon block -> inject -> sign, usual block building process

author: sproul
content: I _think_. I'm a bit fried for today, but will revisit tomorrow.

author: potuz
content: Yes this is correct, proposing was flagged as harder with this approach, I initially thought about caching the state after receiving the Payload and processing it (perhaps with an epoch transition in the middle) but dismissed it as it would make the cache very complex. Perhaps it makes sense to precompute this when we are proposing only

author: terencechain
content:
The issue is you can’t really use `is_parent_block_full` from beacon state helper. You need to compute to know whether, for a given parent root and hash, you are building on a full or empty parent.

But once you know whether you are building on full or empty, the rest seems pretty straightforward right? Nothing really changes there. 

If you are building on empty,  use `state.payload_expected_withdrawals`
If you are building on full, use`get_expected_withdrawals(state)`

author: nflaig
content: `is_parent_block_full` no longer exists

author: terencechain
content: ya good. i havne't checked latest spec.. i did remove most of `is_parent_block_full` from prysm code

content: you where referring to `prepare_execution_payload`?

content: I just updated that today, it should work now, we just consult `get_head`

content: ya. mainly how a proposer should pack withdrawals. Re: sproul's comment

author: potuz
content: no this doesn't work, because you need to call `get_expected_withdrawals(state)` with the post-state of the requests.

wow you are right, i missed that. That's annoying
need to study how to make prysm work with this

author: tbenr
content: <@755590043632140352> is this the caching you were talking about? so you probably want to apply envelop requests immediately to precompute the withdrawals if you are the next proposer so you'll have them already if you decide do build on full?

author: potuz
content: Yes but if we do this our cache becomes actually much more complicated and bug prone as I was complaining early on. I think the computation is fast enough that probably we will be fine without caching that's why I stopped complaining

author: tbenr
content: yes, it seems to me a fast mutation and we don't need to hash the state neither

author: tbenr
content: we need to check on teku 😄

author: terencechain
content: i think the main cost here is you need to copy the state and that's not "free"

author: tbenr
content: copying isn't an issue for us, but materializing changes in validators and balances carried by execution requests might not be that cheap. We will track the performance and evaluate.

author: pawandhananjay
content:
Quick question about the gossip rules for payload bids. The spec says
> [IGNORE] bid.value is less or equal than the builder's excess balance -- i.e. can_builder_cover_bid(state, builder_index, amount) returns True.

What state are we using here? as a proposer, the only useful bids for me would be ones with `parent_block_root` as my canonical head, so would make sense to only check against that state. 
If we start loading states corresponding to the parent, then it opens us up to a dos risk for not much gain. How are other clients handling this?

author: potuz
content: many gossip rules are underspecified in this same regard IMO, I take it that they all refer to the head state of the validating node
